source: iConnections Global Alts 2024 — Industry titans panel: AI, growth/crossover investing
summary: Verticalized AI companies with proprietary data moats are the correct place to invest above the frontier model layer
summary_extended: Baker and Gracias argue that the optimal AI investment strategy above the frontier model layer lies in verticalized companies that possess specific, protectable proprietary data moats — often reinforced by hardware integration — rather than in generic large language model providers. Their reasoning is that vertical AI companies accumulate data advantages that are structurally difficult to replicate: Zipline, for example, has logged millions of proprietary autonomous delivery flight miles, and K Health has amassed unique clinical interaction data, creating defensible competitive positions. Baker contends that this data moat, especially when combined with purpose-built hardware, creates a durable edge that horizontal LLM providers cannot easily commoditize. The investment implication is to favor verticalized AI application-layer companies with proprietary datasets over generic foundation model plays. Both Zipline and K Health are cited as portfolio examples, but both remain private, limiting direct public market expression of this thesis.
detail: Baker and Gracias both argue that the right AI investment strategy is in verticalized companies with specific, protectable data moats — often combined with hardware integration — rather than generic LLM providers. Examples given include Zipline (autonomous delivery with millions of proprietary flight miles) and K Health.
confidence_evidence: Both Baker and Gracias explicitly align on this as their investment framework; multiple portfolio company examples given
quote_fragment: verticalized so they have a very specific data mode that's protectable
